mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction and inventory buildup, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5d returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate falling below 0.50% signals aggressive monetary accommodation that compresses valuation multiples and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%, or the signal generates negative alpha during liquidity traps.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a broad demand shock, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 5d returns, or the joint signal produces positive returns in > 60% of historical recession onsets.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative 20d returns when the economy is in mid-cycle expansion, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
